title: in should be avoided on arrays
description: The 'in' operator when used on arrays checks for the existence of indices, not values. This is often not the intended behavior when developers expect it to check if a value exists in the array. Use Array.includes() to check if a value exists in an array, or use 'in' only when you specifically want to check if an index exists (useful for sparse arrays).
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

compliant: if (0 in sparseArray) { }  // Checking index existence
if ('prop' in myObject) { }  // Objects are OK

checks.message: Use Array.includes() instead of 'in' operator to check array values. The 'in' operator checks indices, not values.
Potential misuse of 'in' operator. If this variable contains an array, use Array.includes() to check values.
